tipRules.tip: KVD: 'The dream scenario for a squarebill in fall is a rocky creek channel swing in the back of a major tributary, with shad active near the surface. It's an efficient bait because bass are moving shallow and will bite it in just about any color of water.'

KVD: 'A 4.5" Sexy Dawg has incredible drawing power — you can attract bass from far away in clear to stained water, as long as there's not too much wave action. Use 50lb braid, 7.3:1 reel — braid makes it easier to walk and gets better hook-ups.'

KVD: 'Not every bass swims to the back of creeks in fall. Keep your boat in 10 feet, cast up to 4-5 feet on shallow flat main lake secondary points. Quality reel for long smooth casts, 12lb fluorocarbon.'

knowledge.insight: KVD: 'Not every quality bass follows bait to the back of a creek in fall — there's still plenty of good fish eating bait on flat, shallow points along the main lake too. Target shallow flat main lake secondary points.'
